skill.name: anatomy-quiz-master
skill.description: Generate interactive anatomy quizzes covering gross anatomy, neuroanatomy, and clinical correlations for medical education and exam preparation.
skill.summary: Interactive anatomy quiz generator for medical students and educators.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 82 static analysis findings. 81 were confirmed false positives: markdown backtick formatting in SKILL.md flagged as shell execution, dictionary .keys() calls matched as cryptographic keys, and a documentation URL matched as a hardcoded endpoint. The only confirmed finding is a legitimate file write at scripts/main.py:243 used for saving quiz output. The skill is a self-contained Python quiz generator with hardcoded question banks, no network access, no credential access, and no code injection vectors.

low_findings.title: File write to user-specified output path
low_findings.description: The script writes quiz output to a file path specified via the --output CLI argument. This is intended functionality for saving quiz results, but the user-controlled path allows writing to any location the process has permission to access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown backtick formatting falsely flagged as shell execution
dangerous_patterns.description: 65 backtick patterns in SKILL.md were flagged as external command execution by the static analyzer. All occurrences are inline code formatting or fenced code blocks in markdown documentation. The skill contains no actual shell command execution beyond documented usage examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dictionary keys method falsely flagged as cryptographic key material
dangerous_patterns.description: Python dict.keys() calls at scripts/main.py:133 and JSON audit field names were matched as certificate or cryptographic key files. These are standard Python dictionary operations and JSON property names with no cryptographic context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation URL falsely flagged as hardcoded network endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub source URL in SKILL.md line 7 is a markdown hyperlink to the source repository. No executable code makes network requests using this URL.

content.user_title: Master Human Anatomy with Interactive AI-Powered Quizzes
content.value_statement: Medical students and educators spend hours creating anatomy quiz materials manually. This skill generates high-quality, clinically-focused anatomy quizzes instantly, saving preparation time while improving learning outcomes.
actual_capabilities: Generate anatomy quiz questions across seven body regions including neuroanatomy
Produce multiple-choice questions with correct answers and clinical explanations
Create quizzes at basic, intermediate, and advanced difficulty levels
Output results in JSON format for LMS integration or plain text for direct use
List available anatomical regions and difficulty levels for quick reference
Export quiz content with clinical correlation notes for each question
limitations: Question bank is limited to hardcoded examples and does not generate novel questions from a knowledge base
Cannot create image-based anatomy identification questions without external image sources
Difficulty levels are labels only and do not adapt question complexity algorithmically
No built-in student tracking or performance analytics across multiple quiz sessions

use_cases.title: Medical Student Self-Assessment
use_cases.description: Students preparing for USMLE or anatomy exams use the skill to generate topic-specific practice quizzes and test their knowledge with immediate answer feedback.
use_cases.target_user: Medical students preparing for anatomy examinations

use_cases.title: Anatomy Instructor Quiz Creation
use_cases.description: Anatomy educators generate quiz materials for classroom use, saving hours of manual question writing while ensuring clinical relevance of each question.
use_cases.target_user: Medical school faculty and anatomy instructors

use_cases.title: Clinical Review and Continuing Education
use_cases.description: Practicing clinicians refresh anatomy knowledge for surgical planning or clinical correlation by generating focused quizzes on specific body regions.
use_cases.target_user: Healthcare professionals seeking anatomy refresher training

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Quiz
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic anatomy quiz with 5 questions about the upper limb.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user asking for a simple anatomy quiz

prompt_templates.title: Specify Region and Difficulty
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 10 advanced-level neuroanatomy questions and save them to a file called neuro_quiz.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced student targeting a specific anatomical region

prompt_templates.title: List Available Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all the anatomical regions and difficulty levels available for quiz generation.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user exploring available quiz topics

prompt_templates.title: Clinical Case Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a thorax quiz with 8 questions at intermediate difficulty that include clinical correlations for each question.
prompt_templates.scenario: Medical educator preparing clinical anatomy materials

output_examples.input: Generate a basic anatomy quiz about the thorax with 3 questions.
output_examples.output: Question: The thoracic duct drains lymph into the: Options are Left subclavian vein, Right subclavian vein, Superior vena cava, or Azygos vein. Correct answer is Left subclavian vein with clinical context about chylothorax risk during surgery.

output_examples.input: Create 2 advanced neuroanatomy questions as a quiz.
output_examples.output: Question: A lesion of the left optic tract results in: Options are Right homonymous hemianopia, Left homonymous hemianopia, Bitemporal hemianopia, or Total blindness left eye. Answer is Right homonymous hemianopia with explanation about visual pathway anatomy.

output_examples.input: Generate an intermediate lower limb anatomy quiz question.
output_examples.output: Question: Which muscle is the primary hip flexor? Options are Iliopsoas, Rectus femoris, Sartorius, or Tensor fasciae latae. Correct answer is Iliopsoas with clinical note about iliopsoas abscess presentation.

best_practices: Always specify the anatomical region and difficulty level for more targeted quiz content.
Review generated questions for anatomical accuracy before using them in formal assessments.
Use the JSON output format when integrating quizzes into learning management systems.
anti_patterns: Do not use generated quizzes as the sole source of truth for medical decision-making or diagnosis.
Avoid generating more questions than the available question bank for a region can support without repetition.
Do not skip clinician review of quiz content before using it in high-stakes examination settings.

faq.question: What anatomical regions does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill covers seven regions: upper limb, lower limb, thorax, abdomen, pelvis, head and neck, and neuroanatomy.

faq.question: Can I export quizzes to my learning management system?
faq.answer: Yes, use the JSON output format with the --output flag to save quiz data that can be imported into most LMS platforms.

faq.question: How are difficulty levels determined?
faq.answer: Difficulty labels of basic, intermediate, and advanced are available but are currently assigned per-request rather than algorithmically calibrated.

faq.question: Does the skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No, the quiz generator runs entirely offline with a built-in question bank and requires no network connection.

faq.question: Can I add my own questions to the question bank?
faq.answer: The question bank is defined within the Python script. You can modify scripts/main.py to add custom questions for your teaching needs.

faq.question: Is the quiz content suitable for USMLE preparation?
faq.answer: Questions are designed with clinical correlations and cover high-yield anatomy topics commonly tested on USMLE Step 1 and NBME exams.
